items.docs: Returns the OpenID Provider Configuration Information document, which contains metadata about the OpenID Provider including its issuer, supported endpoints, scopes, response types, and other capabilities.

params.name: response_type
params.description: The value must include 'code' for the Authorization Code Flow, 'id_token' for the Implicit Flow, or 'code id_token' for the Hybrid Flow.

params.name: client_id
params.description: The client identifier issued during registration.

params.name: redirect_uri
params.description: The redirection URI to which the response will be sent. Must exactly match one of the redirection URIs registered for the client.

params.name: scope
params.description: Space-delimited list of scopes. Must include 'openid' to indicate an OIDC request. May also include 'profile', 'email', 'address', and 'phone'.

params.name: state
params.description: An opaque value used by the client to maintain state between the request and callback. Recommended for CSRF protection.

params.name: nonce
params.description: A string value used to associate a client session with an ID Token and to mitigate replay attacks. Required for implicit flow.

params.name: prompt
params.description: Space-delimited list of values that specifies whether the authorization server prompts the end-user for reauthentication and consent.

params.name: login_hint
params.description: A hint to the authorization server about the login identifier the end-user might use.

params.name: acr_values
params.description: Requested Authentication Context Class Reference values.

params.name: code_challenge
params.description: PKCE code challenge derived from the code verifier.

params.name: code_challenge_method
params.description: Code challenge method used to derive the code challenge.

items.docs: Performs authentication of the end-user. The authorization endpoint is used to interact with the resource owner and obtain an authorization grant. The endpoint handles the authentication flow and redirects back to the client with an authorization code or tokens depending on the response type.

Exchanges an authorization code, refresh token, or client credentials for an access token and optionally a refresh token and ID token. The token endpoint is used by the client to obtain tokens after receiving an authorization code from the authorization endpoint.

Returns claims about the authenticated end-user. The caller must present a valid access token obtained through OIDC authentication. The claims returned depend on the scopes granted during authorization.
Returns claims about the authenticated end-user using a POST request. Functionally identical to the GET variant.
Returns the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) containing the public keys used by the OpenID Provider to sign tokens. Clients use these keys to verify the signatures of ID tokens and other signed artifacts.

params.name: id_token_hint
params.description: The ID token previously issued to the client.

params.name: post_logout_redirect_uri
params.description: The URI to redirect to after logout.

params.name: state
params.description: Opaque value for maintaining state between request and callback.

items.docs: Initiates the logout process. The end-user is redirected to this endpoint to log out of the OpenID Provider. Supports RP-Initiated Logout as defined in the OIDC Session Management specification.
